name: ctxlayer
description: Agent context layer — MCP service on Cloudflare with curated docs, upstream proxy, and usage analytics.
author: Steven Noels and Satisa BV

scripts.dev: concurrently -n worker,web -c blue,green "bun --filter='@ctxlayer/worker' run dev" "bun --filter='@ctxlayer/web' run dev"
scripts.build: bun --filter='@ctxlayer/web' run build && bun --filter='@ctxlayer/worker' run build
scripts.deploy: bun run build && bun --filter='@ctxlayer/worker' run deploy
scripts.deploy:preview: bun run build && bun --filter='@ctxlayer/worker' run deploy:preview
scripts.verify: bun run typecheck && bun run lint && bun run test && bun run test:int
scripts.verify:full: bun run verify && bun run smoke

//zod: No override needed: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk accepts zod '^3.25 || ^4.0', and every workspace now pins ^4, so bun hoists a single zod@4.4.3 that the SDK binds to (its registerTool zod-compat types then match our schemas). The test runner's bundled wrangler@3.100.0/miniflare keeps its own scoped zod@3.25.76 (it calls zod-3-only z.ostring()). Do NOT add a global zod override -> it forces zod 4 onto that test tooling and breaks suite load. A SCOPED 'overrides' on the SDK is also pointless here: bun does not support nested overrides (warns + ignores), and hoisting already does the job.
